  ],
  "text": "The G1 garbage collector's concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle is implemented across multiple interconnected C++ source files in the openjdk/jdk codebase (github.com/sg-evals/jdk--742e735d).\n\n## Concurrent Marking Cycle Start Trigger\nThe cycle is initiated through g1Policy.cpp where initiate_conc_mark() (line 1218) sets the collector state to begin a concurrent start GC pause. This follows the decision logic in decide_on_concurrent_start_pause() (line 1223) which checks if the heap occupancy has exceeded the initiating threshold. The decision is driven by G1Policy tracking heap occupancy metrics and determining when a concurrent marking phase should begin.\n\n## Concurrent Marking Cycle Execution\nThe G1ConcurrentMarkThread (defined in g1ConcurrentMarkThread.hpp) controls the entire marking cycle through concurrent_cycle_start() (g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp, line 267) which delegates to G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start() (g1ConcurrentMark.cpp, line 1114). This function registers timing information and starts the GC trace.\n\nFollowing cycle start, post_concurrent_mark_start() (g1ConcurrentMark.cpp, line 908) activates reference processor discovery and critically, activates the SATB (Snapshot-At-The-Beginning) barrier by calling SATBMarkQueueSet::set_active_all_threads() (satbMarkQueue.cpp, line ~916 via G1BarrierSet). This enables the write barrier to record object references for marking.\n\nThe concurrent_mark_cycle_do() method (g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp, line 271) orchestrates seven phases:\n1. Phase 1: phase_scan_root_regions() (line 172-175) - Scans root regions using G1CMRootRegionScanTask\n2. Phase 2: phase_mark_loop() (line 178-207) - Main marking with mark_from_roots() \n3. Phase 3: phase_rebuild_and_scrub() - Rebuilds remembered sets\n4. Phase 4-7: Cleanup, bitmap clearing, and CLD marking\n\n## SATB Marking Queue Processing\nThe SATB mark queue processing occurs in two key places:\n\n1. During concurrent marking: G1CMTask::drain_satb_buffers() (g1ConcurrentMark.cpp, line 2424) drains SATB buffers from worker threads, pushing their contents onto the global mark stack. This ensures that all references recorded by the write barrier are processed by the concurrent marking phase.\n\n2. The SATBMarkQueueSet (satbMarkQueue.hpp, line 86) manages the global queue set with methods:\n   - set_active_all_threads() - Activates the SATB barrier on all threads\n   - apply_closure_to_completed_buffer() (line 139) - Processes completed SATB buffers\n   - set_process_completed_buffers_threshold() (line 130) - Controls when buffers are flushed\n\n## Remembered Set Scanning\nDuring the concurrent marking and collection phases, the remembered set scanning is implemented in G1RemSet (g1RemSet.hpp and cpp). The scan_heap_roots() method (g1RemSet.hpp, line 86-90) scans cards in non-collection-set regions that contain references into the collection set.\n\nThe G1RemSetScanState class (g1RemSet.cpp, line 89-271) manages the state of remembered set scanning, including:\n- Tracking which regions have been scanned\n- Organizing cards into \"chunks\" for parallel scanning\n- Maintaining dirty region information for efficient scanning\n\n## Mixed Collection Region Selection\nAfter concurrent marking completes, old regions are selected for mixed collections through G1CollectionSet::finalize_initial_collection_set() (g1CollectionSet.cpp, line 666-668) which calls select_candidates_from_marking() (line 380-460).\n\nThis selection process:\n1. Retrieves candidates from the G1CollectionSetCandidates structure populated during marking\n2. Calculates minimum and maximum old region counts using G1Policy's calc_min_old_cset_length() and calc_max_old_cset_length()\n3. Iterates through G1CSetCandidateGroup objects in priority order (organized during marking based on reclamation value)\n4. Adds groups to the collection set until reaching time constraints or region limits\n5. Supports adaptive selection with optional regions when time permits\n\nThe marked candidates are organized by G1CollectionSetCandidates (in g1CollectionSet.hpp) with from_marking_groups() providing the list of old regions to consider for evacuation.\n\n## Cycle Interconnection\nThe complete cycle chain flows: G1Policy initiates -> G1ConcurrentMarkThread controls phases -> G1ConcurrentMark executes marking -> SATB barrier records references -> Marking queues drain SATB buffers -> G1CMTask processes objects -> Remembered sets prepared -> G1CollectionSet selects candidates for mixed GC -> Mixed collections evacuate marked old regions.\n\nAll of these components work together to implement G1's concurrent marking and mixed collection strategy, enabling low-latency concurrent marking while maintaining efficient heap compaction through periodic mixed evacuations of loosely-populated old regions."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# OpenJDK G1 GC Garbage Collection Cycle Chain

## Your Task

Trace the G1 garbage collector's concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in openjdk/jdk. Find C++ source files implementing: the G1ConcurrentMark start trigger, the SATB marking queue processing, the remembered set scanning, and the mixed collection region selection.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossrepo domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/jdk--742e735d.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/jdk--742e735d` (openjdk/jdk)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you trace the G1 garbage collector's concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in openjdk/jdk. Let me start by discovering the repository and searching for the relevant C++ source files.
Now let me read some of the key files to understand the implementation details better:
Now let me create the comprehensive answer.json file with all the findings:

Perfect! I've created a comprehensive `answer.json` file that traces the G1 garbage collector's concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in the openjdk/jdk repository.

## Summary of Findings

The answer includes:

### **17 Key C++ Source Files** implementing:
- **G1ConcurrentMark** (hpp/cpp) - Main concurrent marking class managing the cycle
- **G1ConcurrentMarkThread** (hpp/cpp) - Thread controlling marking phases
- **G1Policy** (hpp/cpp) - Triggers marking cycle based on heap occupancy
- **G1RemSet** (hpp/cpp) - Remembers set scanning during collection
- **G1CollectionSet** (hpp/cpp) - Selects old regions for mixed collection
- **SATBMarkQueue** (hpp/cpp) - Snapshot-At-The-Beginning barrier implementation
- **G1BarrierSet** (hpp/cpp) - Write barrier integration
- And supporting infrastructure files

### **Key Components in the Execution Chain**:

1. **Cycle Trigger**: `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()` - Decides when to start based on heap occupancy
2. **Cycle Start**: `G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start()` - Initializes GC timing
3. **SATB Activation**: `SATBMarkQueueSet::set_active_all_threads()` - Enables write barrier
4. **Root Region Scan**: `G1ConcurrentMark::scan_root_regions()` - Initial scanning phase
5. **Main Marking**: `G1ConcurrentMark::mark_from_roots()` - Concurrent marking with parallel tasks
6. **Queue Drainage**: `G1CMTask::drain_satb_buffers()` - Processes references from SATB barrier
7. **RemSet Scanning**: `G1RemSet::scan_heap_roots()` - Scans remembered sets
8. **Region Selection**: `G1CollectionSet::select_candidates_from_marking()` - Chooses old regions for mixed GC

The file is ready at `/workspace/answer.json`!

  "text": "The G1 garbage collector's concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle is implemented across multiple interconnected C++ source files in the openjdk/jdk codebase (github.com/sg-evals/jdk--742e735d).\n\n## Concurrent Marking Cycle Start Trigger\nThe cycle is initiated through g1Policy.cpp where initiate_conc_mark() (line 1218) sets the collector state to begin a concurrent start GC pause. This follows the decision logic in decide_on_concurrent_start_pause() (line 1223) which checks if the heap occupancy has exceeded the initiating threshold. The decision is driven by G1Policy tracking heap occupancy metrics and determining when a concurrent marking phase should begin.\n\n## Concurrent Marking Cycle Execution\nThe G1ConcurrentMarkThread (defined in g1ConcurrentMarkThread.hpp) controls the entire marking cycle through concurrent_cycle_start() (g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp, line 267) which delegates to G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start() (g1ConcurrentMark.cpp, line 1114). This function registers timing information and starts the GC trace.\n\nFollowing cycle start, post_concurrent_mark_start() (g1ConcurrentMark.cpp, line 908) activates reference processor discovery and critically, activates the SATB (Snapshot-At-The-Beginning) barrier by calling SATBMarkQueueSet::set_active_all_threads() (satbMarkQueue.cpp, line ~916 via G1BarrierSet). This enables the write barrier to record object references for marking.\n\nThe concurrent_mark_cycle_do() method (g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp, line 271) orchestrates seven phases:\n1. Phase 1: phase_scan_root_regions() (line 172-175) - Scans root regions using G1CMRootRegionScanTask\n2. Phase 2: phase_mark_loop() (line 178-207) - Main marking with mark_from_roots() \n3. Phase 3: phase_rebuild_and_scrub() - Rebuilds remembered sets\n4. Phase 4-7: Cleanup, bitmap clearing, and CLD marking\n\n## SATB Marking Queue Processing\nThe SATB mark queue processing occurs in two key places:\n\n1. During concurrent marking: G1CMTask::drain_satb_buffers() (g1ConcurrentMark.cpp, line 2424) drains SATB buffers from worker threads, pushing their contents onto the global mark stack. This ensures that all references recorded by the write barrier are processed by the concurrent marking phase.\n\n2. The SATBMarkQueueSet (satbMarkQueue.hpp, line 86) manages the global queue set with methods:\n   - set_active_all_threads() - Activates the SATB barrier on all threads\n   - apply_closure_to_completed_buffer() (line 139) - Processes completed SATB buffers\n   - set_process_completed_buffers_threshold() (line 130) - Controls when buffers are flushed\n\n## Remembered Set Scanning\nDuring the concurrent marking and collection phases, the remembered set scanning is implemented in G1RemSet (g1RemSet.hpp and cpp). The scan_heap_roots() method (g1RemSet.hpp, line 86-90) scans cards in non-collection-set regions that contain references into the collection set.\n\nThe G1RemSetScanState class (g1RemSet.cpp, line 89-271) manages the state of remembered set scanning, including:\n- Tracking which regions have been scanned\n- Organizing cards into \"chunks\" for parallel scanning\n- Maintaining dirty region information for efficient scanning\n\n## Mixed Collection Region Selection\nAfter concurrent marking completes, old regions are selected for mixed collections through G1CollectionSet::finalize_initial_collection_set() (g1CollectionSet.cpp, line 666-668) which calls select_candidates_from_marking() (line 380-460).\n\nThis selection process:\n1. Retrieves candidates from the G1CollectionSetCandidates structure populated during marking\n2. Calculates minimum and maximum old region counts using G1Policy's calc_min_old_cset_length() and calc_max_old_cset_length()\n3. Iterates through G1CSetCandidateGroup objects in priority order (organized during marking based on reclamation value)\n4. Adds groups to the collection set until reaching time constraints or region limits\n5. Supports adaptive selection with optional regions when time permits\n\nThe marked candidates are organized by G1CollectionSetCandidates (in g1CollectionSet.hpp) with from_marking_groups() providing the list of old regions to consider for evacuation.\n\n## Cycle Interconnection\nThe complete cycle chain flows: G1Policy initiates -> G1ConcurrentMarkThread controls phases -> G1ConcurrentMark executes marking -> SATB barrier records references -> Marking queues drain SATB buffers -> G1CMTask processes objects -> Remembered sets prepared -> G1CollectionSet selects candidates for mixed GC -> Mixed collections evacuate marked old regions.\n\nAll of these components work together to implement G1's concurrent marking and mixed collection strategy, enabling low-latency concurrent marking while maintaining efficient heap compaction through periodic mixed evacuations of loosely-populated old regions."
}
